ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION HAVE BEEN WITHDRAWN EXCEPT FOR ISSUES 5 AND 7. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO PREMISES OWNER/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY (ISSUE 5) AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES (ISSUE 7). METALCLAD SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION BY PRESENTING PLAINTIFFS' FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY RESPONSES ON THESE ISSUES. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE SUFFICIENT TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT THAT METALCLAD: 1) MANAGED OR CONTROLLED ANY PREMISES WHERE MR. CLARK WORKED, 2) INSTRUCTED MR. CLARK REGARDING THE PERFORMANCE OF HIS WORK, OR 3) ACTED WITH MALICE, FRAUD, OR OPPRESSION BY CLEAR AND CONVINCING EVIDENCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: HELEN SOREP-MATLEY, CSR 	225

PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT BY 12/3/09. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES AFTER THE DUE DATE THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT IDENTIFIED PREVIOUSLY. ALL DEFENDANTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL SUPPORT PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 12/23/09. IF DEFENDANTS LEARN OF NEW WITNESSES AND OR DOCUMENTS, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED PREVIOUSLY. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE E-SERVED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lisa Oberg, Esq. of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4127), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
David L. Amell, Esq. of PAUL & HANLEY (510-559-9980) X-135, appearing for plaintiff / opposing party George S. Clark

RULING:
Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary judgment and/or adjudication. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for summary adjudication have been withdrawn except for issues 5 and 7. The motion is granted as to premises owner/contractor liability (issue 5) and punitive damages (issue 7). Metalclad satisfied its burden of production by presenting plaintiffs' factually devoid discovery responses on these issues. Plaintiffs have not presented evidence sufficient to create a triable issue of fact that Metalclad: 1) managed or controlled any premises where Mr. Clark worked, 2) instructed Mr. Clark regarding the performance of his work, or 3) acted with malice, fraud, or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225
